scope_note: QA Historical Nav Cert family [167]: certifies that historical navigation systems across 5 civilizations used integer-ratio methods structurally equivalent to QA arithmetic. Plimpton 322 (Babylon ~1800BCE) = Berggren tree direction table. Polynesian star compass (32 houses) = mod-32 discrete bearing system. Norse sun stones = spread measurement via birefringence. Arab kamal = integer spread increments (finger-widths). Egyptian seked = integer slope ratio = spread. Each system operates on discrete integer states with observer projection at measurement — proto-QA navigation. Tier 2 structural correspondence.
